DP.071.0000:
Hello! Welcome to the Oreburgh
Mining Museum!
DP.071.0001:
Hello, hello!
How are you?\f
I study Pokémon Fossils!
Me! Right here and now!\f
DP.071.0002:
You have a Fossil for me, yes?
Shall I turn it into a Pokémon?
DP.071.0003:
Which Fossil should I turn back
into a Pokémon? Choose! Now!
DP.071.0004:
OK, I will do that! Turn that Fossil
back into a Pokémon, surely I will!
DP.071.0005:
You don’t wish that I extract any
Pokémon from a Fossil. Fine, then!
DP.071.0006:
I am in the process of extracting
your Pokémon from a Fossil.\f
You go outside, yes, please. Now!
Time alone is what I now need. NOW!!!
DP.071.0007:
You were gone too long!
You kept us waiting! Unacceptable!\f
This is your [0100,0000]!
You be good to it, OK!\f
DP.071.0009:
Give a nickname to the [0100,0001]
you received?
DP.071.0010:
But, no, I cannot do this!
You have too many Pokémon with you!\f
I must hold it for you, so come back,
you, and take it away, please.
DP.071.0011:
If it is a Fossil that you obtain,
bring it to me if you must.
DP.071.0012:
On the verge of a breakthrough,
I am, to achieve extraction from\r
Fossils.\f
From Fossils I can extract Pokémon,
but not yet, not yet.
DP.071.0013:
Whoa! So this is coal?
I never expected something this big!
DP.071.0014:
Wow! The Pokémon that carried this
must be terribly strong!
DP.071.0015:
Well, how about that!\f
The Oreburgh Mine extends
under the seafloor!
DP.071.0016:
How Coal Is Made   1\f
“In a time long before history,
vegetative matter was swept along\f
by rain and rivers. It came to be
buried under the ground.”
DP.071.0017:
How Coal Is Made   2\f
“The buried plant matter was
forced ever deeper under the ground\f
due to tremors and fissures caused
by the shifting landmasses.”
DP.071.0018:
How Coal Is Made   3\f
“Deep under the ground, the
plant matter was subjected to heavy\f
pressure and the heat of magma.
It gradually turned into coal.”
DP.071.0019:
Samples of coal from different regions
are displayed.
DP.071.0020:
The box contains categorized samples
of coal.
DP.071.0021:
Different kinds of lamps are
displayed.\f
They appear to have been worn
on someone’s head while underground.
DP.071.0022:
Badly scarred wooden tools are
displayed.\f
They apparently dug out coal using
these tools in the olden days.
DP.071.0023:
The everyday items and tools of
past mine workers are displayed.
